title: California Democrats play it safe with Becerra, defying national trends
description: In the complicated governor’s race, voters embraced the former health secretary’s centrist stance over the passionate promises of insurgent candidates.

title: Hilton, Becerra lead in California’s governor primary. See live results here.
description: Latest California governor primary results as Democrats Katie Porter, Tom Steyer and Xavier Becerra and Republicans Chad Bianco, Steve Hilton vie for nominations to replace Gov. Gavin Newsom.

title: Former Biden official Deb Haaland wins New Mexico primary for governor
description: Haaland could make history as the first female Native American governor if she’s elected.

title: National Guard has done little to reduce violent crime in D.C., a new study finds
description: A new study has found that the National Guard's presence in Washington, D.C. had no effect on violent crime in the city. The Guard has been deployed since last August as part of a federal task force to fight crime, and their numbers are set to double in the coming weeks.

title: In photos: a preview of the Obama Presidential Center
description: The Obama Presidential Center opens later this month in Chicago. We take a look inside.

title: Leon Panetta on Trump's controversial pick for acting national intelligence director
description: NPR's Leila Fadel speaks with former Defense Secretary and CIA Director Leon Panetta about President Trump's controversial appointment of Bill Pulte as acting director of national intelligence.

title: Divisions between GOP and Trump surface over 'anti-weaponization fund' and Iran war
description: Some Republicans' frustration with President Trump has been on display in Congress this week, first with Trump's "anti-weaponization fund" and then the war with Iran.

title: Rep. Katherine Clark on the House passing war powers resolution to end Iran war
description: NPR's Steve Inskeep asks Democratic Whip Katherine Clark of Massachusetts about House approval of a war powers resolution directing President Trump to pull U.S. forces from hostilities with Iran.

title: Trump signs executive order that makes it easier to fire federal employees
description: President Trump signed an executive order reclassifying 8,000 high-ranking civil servants into at-will employees who can be fired without due process.

title: President Trump says he will nominate Todd Blanche to serve as attorney general
description: Trump said at a dinner at the White House that he plans to nominate Blanche formally, according to a video of the event posted on social media by a White House aide.

title: What to know about Trump’s immunity deal with the IRS
description: It’s “a legal absurdity,” said one expert.

title: House passes war powers resolution directing Trump to end hostilities with Iran
description: The vote marked a rare bipartisan rebuke of the war, but is mostly symbolic. Democrats have been unable to pass a war powers resolution in the Senate, and even if they could it would likely be vetoed.

title: Senate Republicans start debate on ICE funding package
description: The Senate voted along party lines to start debate on a Republican bill to fund immigration enforcement through the end of President Trump's term.

title: Rep. Ogles deletes homophobic tweet, blames staff amid rare GOP pushback
description: A post on Ogles’s official X account Tuesday stated: “Homosexuality has no place in America. Happy Nuclear Family Month.”

title: Voters are sending a message to L.A.’s Democratic mayor
description: No incumbent mayor of the country's second-largest city has faced a runoff in more than two decades.

title: Trump strips job protections from 8,000 federal workers
description: President Trump signed an executive order that puts some 8,000 high-ranking civil servants into a new category of employees who can be fired for any reason.

title: Pentagon hires convicted Jan. 6 rioter for sensitive counterterrorism job
description: The Trump administration’s appointment of Elias Irizarry, who was 19 at the time of the Capitol attack, has alarmed some in the Defense Department.

title: Nine takeaways from a big primary night in Iowa, California and more
description: In Iowa, Josh Turek won the Democratic nomination for Senate, defeating a rival who had criticized party leaders. Major races in California were not yet called.

title: Tracking who Trump is appointing to fill key administration roles
description: Follow President Trump’s progress filling over 800 positions, among about 1,300 that require Senate confirmation, in this tracker from The Washington Post and the Partnership for Public Service.

title: Centrist Democrats launch new pledge: ‘We are capitalist, not socialist’
description: A pair of Democratic House members who won Republican-held seats want others to sign a vow for moderate policies.

title: America’s 250th birthday celebration increasingly centers on Trump
description: The White House has built a set of highly visible, centrally controlled events — many created by presidential directive — that emphasize spectacle, branding, and new traditions.

title: Bass advances in Los Angeles mayor race. See primary results live.
description: Latest Los Angeles mayoral primary results, as incumbent Karen Bass seeks reelection in a tight race with candidates Nithya Raman and Spencer Pratt.

title: What Jill Biden’s memoir says about Melania, the East Wing and that debate
description: The former first lady defends her husband's 2024 decisions and capabilities in a book that also touches on Hunter Biden's trial, Barron Trump and taking up mah-jongg.

title: Rubio details U.S. demands as Iran peace talks falter
description: The secretary of state is testifying publicly to Congress for the first time since the war began, as the regional conflict worsens and lawmakers’ patience runs thin.
